Your server's coding agent

Ship code from your
Discord server.

One shared AI engineer for your whole community — and it signs its work. Type /code in any channel; every PR ships with a named human sponsor and a provenance receipt.

See how it works

BYO LLM key · Isolated containers · Never pushes to main

#task-128↻ replay
you/code add dark-mode toggle
→ opened thread #task-128
→ pushed anywherecode/ab12cd
✓ opened PR #128

Plugs into the tools you already use

GitHub
Discord
Claude
/codeSpawn a thread, work the task, open a PR
/askRepo-aware Q&A, read-only
/connect githubLink your GitHub repos
/connect llmBring your own LLM key
/setupConnection status + usage
/repo setPick the active repo for a channel
/statusRunning and queued tasks
/config roleChoose who may invoke the agent
/codeSpawn a thread, work the task, open a PR
/askRepo-aware Q&A, read-only
/connect githubLink your GitHub repos
/connect llmBring your own LLM key
/setupConnection status + usage
/repo setPick the active repo for a channel
/statusRunning and queued tasks
/config roleChoose who may invoke the agent
Capabilities

Everything your server needs to ship

A shared coding agent that lives in Discord — from a one-line task to a reviewed pull request, with the guardrails baked in.

⌗

/code → pull request

Type a task in any channel. The agent opens a thread, works in an isolated container, pushes a branch, and opens a PR with Merge / Iterate buttons.

?

/ask, repo-aware

Read-only Q&A grounded in the connected repo. Looser monthly cap than /code because it never writes.

⇄

Shared threads

Anyone in the thread can steer mid-task. Replies forward straight into the live agent as new turns — pair-program as a team.

@

@mention routing

Tag the bot anywhere. One classifier call routes to a plain reply, an /ask, a /code run, or a durable proposal with Run / Dismiss buttons.

⚷

Bring your own LLM

Each server connects its own credential — Anthropic API key, a Claude Pro/Max subscription token, or any Anthropic-compatible endpoint. Encrypted at rest.

▣

Hardened by default

Ephemeral non-root containers, read-only tokens for verification runs, and a quarantine layer that strips hidden instructions from inbound issues — designed after Comment and Control, not before it.

🔬

Repro Gate

Inbound bug reports get verified in the sandbox before a human spends a minute: symbols checked, snippets run, a verdict card posted. Your slop filter, not another slop source.

🧾

Provenance receipts

Every agent PR names its human sponsor, plan approver, and steerers — with test evidence and a link to the public thread. The only AI contribution maintainers can audit.

⚔

Squad Mode

Hard problem? Run N parallel attempts in separate sandboxes, compare diffs side by side, and let the server vote on which one ships.

🔌

MCP extensions

Attach your Sentry, database, or tracker via Model Context Protocol — the agent runs with your context, role-gated and egress-allowlisted per connection.

How it works

From a message to a merged PR

Four steps. No setup ceremony.

01
01

Add to Discord

Invite the bot with one click. It registers its slash commands automatically on boot.

Add to Discord →
02
02

Connect repo + key

Link a GitHub repo and bring your own LLM credential. Both are scoped per server.

›/connect github
›/connect llm
03
03

Type /code

Describe the change in any channel. A thread opens and the agent streams its progress live.

›/code add dark-mode toggle to settings
04
04

Review the PR

It pushes a branch and opens a pull request. Merge, or hit Iterate to keep going — never touches main.

✓ PR #128 opened
Security

Locked down by default

Repo content is untrusted, every task runs in an isolated, auto-removed container, and your bring-your-own LLM key is encrypted at rest. Defense in depth, not an afterthought.

Read the FAQ →

Isolated by construction

Ephemeral, non-root, egress-locked.

Linux capabilitiesALL dropped
Filesystemephemeral · auto-removed
Network (prod)Anthropic + GitHub only
Keys at restAES-256-GCM · per-server

Repo content is untrusted

The agent is told to ignore instructions embedded in repo files — a prompt-injection defense baked into its system prompt.

Credentials never leak

Tokens travel over stdin, never as container env vars, and are stripped from every error path before any text can reach Discord.

AES-256-GCM at rest

Stored LLM keys are encrypted with a per-server key derived via HKDF; a guild's blob can't be copied to another server.

Isolated execution

Containers drop all Linux capabilities, run non-root with CPU/mem/PID caps, auto-remove, and in prod can only reach Anthropic + GitHub.

Never pushes to main

Git happens in one place. The bot opens PRs on branch anywherecode/<taskId>; nothing ever pushes to your default branch.

You control access

Admins-only by default. Pick exactly which role may invoke the agent with /config role.

Pricing

Simple, per-server pricing

One subscription for the whole server — no per-seat licenses. 14-day trial, then bring your own LLM key.

OSS Community

$0/mo

For verified public open-source servers. Your runs are the demo.

  • 30 pooled code tasks / mo
  • Unlimited questions on public repos
  • Repro Gate — filter slop reports free
  • Maintainer-gated runs
  • Apply with /oss apply
Add to Discord →
Most popular

Pro

$20/mo

One shared engineer for the whole server — no per-seat math.

  • 100 code tasks / mo
  • 400 questions / mo + Repro Gate
  • 2 concurrent tasks
  • Server Memory + Review agent
  • Scheduled tasks + MCP extensions
Get Pro →

Studio

$50/mo

For studios living in voice channels and shipping daily.

  • 500 code tasks / mo
  • 2,000 questions / mo
  • 5 concurrent tasks
  • Voice → PR Standup Mode
  • Squad Mode + Spectate + previews
Get Studio →

🔋 Task Pack — $10: 50 extra code tasks for the server, buyable by ANY member — Discord-boost style, with public credit. Never expires while subscribed.

Paid plans check out per server from your dashboard after you sign in with Discord. Caps reset monthly.

FAQ

Questions, answered

No. Each task clones into an ephemeral container that is auto-removed when it exits. We persist only task history and usage counters — removing the bot deletes your server's data.

Yours. Every server connects its own credential, encrypted per-server with AES-256-GCM. The 14-day trial runs on a small platform allowance; after that, bring your own. An Anthropic API key is the recommended path for production servers.

Community-funded compute: any member can buy 50 extra code tasks for the server ($10), Discord-boost style — with public credit in the server. Packs sit in reserve and are spent after the monthly plan cap.

The trial runs on our key, so it's gated against farming: the server must be at least 30 days old with 5+ human members, and each GitHub org gets one trial. Connecting your own key skips all gates.

An Anthropic API key, a Claude Pro/Max subscription token (claude setup-token), or any Anthropic-compatible endpoint such as a LiteLLM proxy.

Never. All git activity is confined to one place — it pushes to anywherecode/<taskId> and opens a pull request. Your default branch is never touched.

Admins only by default. Use /config role to grant a specific role access. @everyone and @here never trigger the bot.

All external content is treated as untrusted. Inbound issues and PRs pass a quarantine layer that strips HTML comments and invisible Unicode and flags instruction-like content on the card; verification runs hold read-only tokens; and the container is fully isolated. Designed after the Comment and Control disclosures, not before them.

The opposite, by construction: nothing runs without a named human sponsor, every PR carries a provenance receipt (who asked, who approved, who steered, what was verified), and Repro Gate filters incoming bug reports before they cost a human minute.

The open per-repo conventions standard read by 20+ coding tools. AnywhereCode reads yours on every run, and /memory commit flows your server's accumulated conventions back into it via PR — your context improves every agent you use, not just ours.

Give your server a coding agent.

Add AnywhereCode in a click. 14-day free trial, then bring your own key.
